tasks.title: Design and Deploy a Cloud-Native Microservice
tasks.description: Develop a simple REST API microservice (e.g., user management) using Python/Java, containerize it with Docker, and deploy it to a Kubernetes cluster (e.g., Minikube/EKS/AKS/GKE).
tasks.expected_output: Running microservice accessible via Kubernetes, Dockerfile, and K8s YAMLs in a Git repo.

tasks.title: Implement Secure API Gateway with OWASP Best Practices
tasks.description: Set up an API Gateway (e.g., AWS API Gateway, Nginx, Kong) in front of a simple backend service. Configure it to apply security policies based on OWASP Top 10 (e.g., rate limiting, input validation, authentication).
requirements: Protect against Injection, XSS, and Broken Authentication
tasks.expected_output: Secure API Gateway protecting a dummy endpoint, configuration files, and test cases demonstrating protection.

tasks.title: Build and Deploy Infrastructure as Code for a Web App
tasks.description: Use Terraform to provision the necessary cloud infrastructure (e.g., VPC, subnets, EC2 instance/VM, security groups, database) for a basic web application on AWS/Azure/GCP.
tasks.expected_output: Deployed cloud infrastructure via Terraform, Terraform configuration files, and a running web server.

tasks.title: Simulate a Distributed System Failure and Recovery
tasks.description: Set up a simple distributed application (e.g., a producer-consumer setup with a message queue like Kafka/SQS). Introduce a failure (e.g., stop a consumer, network partition) and implement a recovery mechanism.
requirements: Implement retry logic or dead-letter queue
tasks.expected_output: Documented failure simulation and recovery process, code for the distributed app.

tasks.title: Cloud Security Audit and Remediation Plan
tasks.description: Perform a basic security audit of an existing cloud environment (or a simulated one). Identify potential vulnerabilities related to IAM, network configuration, and data storage. Propose remediation steps.
requirements: Use cloud provider's security tools (e.g., AWS Security Hub, Azure Security Center)
tasks.expected_output: A report detailing findings and a remediation plan.

roadmap.task: Understand 12-Factor App, microservices principles, and containerization concepts.
Hands-on with Docker: build, run, and manage containers.
Introduction to Kubernetes architecture, pods, deployments, and services.
Study CAP theorem, consistency models, and fault tolerance patterns.
Explore Kafka/RabbitMQ/SQS for inter-service communication in distributed systems.
Learn about Infrastructure as Code principles, declarative vs. imperative.
Hands-on with Terraform: provision basic resources (VPC, EC2) on AWS/Azure/GCP.
Deep dive into common web vulnerabilities and their impact.
Learn best practices for preventing OWASP vulnerabilities in code.
Focus on IAM, network security, data encryption in cloud environments.
Review common security domains for specialist certifications (e.g., incident response, compliance).
Explore multi-region deployments, serverless patterns, and cost optimization.
Apply learned concepts to solve a complex distributed system design problem.
Consolidate knowledge, practice interview questions, and review key concepts.

questions.question: Which of the following is a common strategy for scaling a database horizontally?
options: A. Increasing RAM on a single server
C. Upgrading to a faster CPU
D. Using a single, larger disk
questions.explanation: Sharding distributes data across multiple database instances for scalability.

questions.question: To handle high traffic, which component is typically used to distribute incoming requests across multiple servers?
questions.explanation: Load balancers distribute traffic, preventing single server overload and improving availability.

questions.question: Implementing a ___ layer can significantly reduce database load by storing frequently accessed data.
questions.explanation: Caching stores data, reducing database queries and improving response times.

questions.question: Explain the difference between vertical and horizontal scaling in system architecture.
questions.correct_answer: Vertical scaling adds resources to one machine; horizontal adds more machines.
questions.explanation: Vertical scales up, horizontal scales out, distributing load.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Kubernetes in container orchestration?
options: B. To manage and automate containerized applications
questions.correct_answer: B. To manage and automate containerized applications
questions.explanation: Kubernetes automates deployment, scaling, and management of containerized workloads.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a core component of a Kubernetes cluster?
questions.explanation: Dockerfile defines images; Pods, Nodes, Services are Kubernetes runtime components.

questions.question: A Kubernetes ___ is the smallest deployable unit that can be created and managed.
questions.explanation: A Pod encapsulates one or more containers, storage, and network resources.

questions.question: Describe the role of a Deployment in Kubernetes and how it manages Pods.
questions.correct_answer: A Deployment manages replica sets, ensuring a desired number of Pods are running.
questions.explanation: It handles rolling updates, rollbacks, and self-healing for applications.

questions.question: What is the main benefit of using GitHub Actions or GitLab CI for CI/CD?
options: B. Automated testing and deployment workflows
questions.correct_answer: B. Automated testing and deployment workflows
questions.explanation: CI/CD automates building, testing, and deploying code changes efficiently.

questions.question: In GitHub Actions, what file defines the automated workflow?
questions.explanation: Workflow definitions are YAML files located in the .github/workflows directory.

questions.question: A ___ is a configurable automated process that runs one or more jobs in GitHub Actions.
questions.explanation: Workflows define the sequence of steps and jobs for CI/CD.

questions.question: Explain the concept of 'jobs' and 'steps' within a GitHub Actions workflow.
questions.correct_answer: Jobs are independent tasks that run in parallel or sequentially. Steps are commands within a job.
questions.explanation: Jobs group steps; steps are individual actions or shell commands.

questions.question: Which quality is most crucial for an effective technical mentor?
options: B. Active listening and guiding questions
C. Focusing solely on code reviews
questions.correct_answer: B. Active listening and guiding questions
questions.explanation: Mentors empower mentees to find solutions, fostering growth.

questions.question: What is a primary benefit for a mentee in a technical mentorship relationship?
options: B. Accelerated learning and career development
D. Exclusive access to company secrets
questions.correct_answer: B. Accelerated learning and career development
questions.explanation: Mentorship provides guidance, insights, and support for professional growth.

questions.question: A good technical mentor helps mentees develop ___ skills rather than just providing answers.
questions.explanation: Mentors foster independent thinking and skill development.

questions.question: How can a technical mentor effectively provide constructive feedback?
questions.correct_answer: Focus on specific behaviors, offer solutions, and maintain a supportive tone.
questions.explanation: Feedback should be actionable, timely, and delivered respectfully.

questions.question: What is a core principle of Systems Thinking?
options: A. Analyzing individual components in isolation
B. Understanding interconnections and emergent properties
C. Focusing only on immediate causes
questions.correct_answer: B. Understanding interconnections and emergent properties
questions.explanation: Systems thinking emphasizes holistic understanding of complex interactions.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes an 'emergent property' in a system?
options: A. A property of an individual component
B. A property that arises from the interaction of components, not present in isolation
C. A property that is easily predicted from component analysis
D. A property that is irrelevant to system behavior
questions.correct_answer: B. A property that arises from the interaction of components, not present in isolation
questions.explanation: Emergent properties are characteristics of the whole system, not its parts.

questions.question: In Systems Thinking, a ___ loop can either amplify or dampen changes within a system.
questions.explanation: Feedback loops are crucial for understanding system dynamics and behavior.

questions.question: How does understanding feedback loops contribute to effective system design?
questions.correct_answer: It helps predict system behavior, identify leverage points, and prevent unintended consequences.
questions.explanation: Feedback loops reveal how changes propagate, aiding stability and control.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of the OWASP Top 10 list?
options: A. To provide a definitive list of all known vulnerabilities
B. To raise awareness about the most critical web application security risks
C. To offer a complete guide to penetration testing
D. To certify secure software applications
questions.correct_answer: B. To raise awareness about the most critical web application security risks
questions.explanation: OWASP Top 10 highlights common, impactful web security flaws for developers.

questions.question: Which OWASP Top 10 category involves sending untrusted data to an interpreter as part of a command or query?
questions.explanation: Injection flaws allow attackers to execute unintended commands or access data.

questions.question: ___ is an OWASP Top 10 vulnerability where an attacker can execute arbitrary client-side scripts in a victim's browser.
questions.explanation: XSS allows attackers to inject malicious scripts into web pages viewed by others.

questions.question: Briefly explain how 'Broken Access Control' (OWASP Top 10) can be exploited and how to mitigate it.
questions.correct_answer: Exploited by unauthorized users accessing functions/data. Mitigate with strict access policies.
questions.explanation: Ensure proper authentication, authorization, and least privilege principles.

questions.question: Which database scaling technique involves distributing data across multiple machines based on a key?
questions.explanation: Sharding distributes data across multiple database instances for scalability.

questions.question: To handle sudden traffic spikes for a web application, which strategy is most effective?
options: C. Implementing auto-scaling and load balancing
questions.correct_answer: C. Implementing auto-scaling and load balancing
questions.explanation: Auto-scaling and load balancing dynamically adjust resources for traffic.

questions.question: A Content Delivery Network (CDN) primarily helps improve ___ by caching content closer to users.
questions.explanation: CDNs reduce latency by serving content from edge locations.

questions.question: Describe the challenges of maintaining consistency in a distributed system at scale.
questions.correct_answer: Ensuring all nodes have same data, handling network partitions, and managing concurrent updates.
questions.explanation: Challenges include data synchronization, network issues, and concurrent updates.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Kubernetes Pod?
options: C. To run one or more containers as a single unit
questions.correct_answer: C. To run one or more containers as a single unit
questions.explanation: Pods are the smallest deployable units in Kubernetes, encapsulating containers.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a benefit of containerization?
options: C. Reduced resource utilization compared to VMs
D. Elimination of all security vulnerabilities
questions.correct_answer: D. Elimination of all security vulnerabilities
questions.explanation: Containerization improves security, but doesn't eliminate all vulnerabilities.

questions.question: Kubernetes uses ___ to automatically distribute incoming requests among multiple instances of an application.
questions.explanation: Kubernetes Services provide stable network endpoints for Pods.

questions.question: Explain the role of a Deployment in Kubernetes and how it manages application lifecycle.
questions.correct_answer: A Deployment manages replica sets, ensuring desired state, enabling rolling updates and rollbacks.
questions.explanation: Deployments manage application updates, scaling, and self-healing in Kubernetes.

questions.question: In GitHub Actions, what defines the automated process that runs in response to an event?
questions.explanation: Workflows are automated processes defined in YAML files in GitHub Actions.

questions.question: Which of these is a key advantage of using CI/CD pipelines?
options: A. Eliminates need for version control
B. Speeds up software delivery and reduces errors
questions.correct_answer: B. Speeds up software delivery and reduces errors
questions.explanation: CI/CD automates integration and delivery, improving speed and quality.

questions.question: GitHub Actions workflows are typically defined in YAML files located in the ___ directory.
questions.explanation: Workflow definitions reside in the .github/workflows directory.

questions.question: Describe how you would set up a basic CI pipeline for a Python application using GitHub Actions.
questions.correct_answer: Create YAML in .github/workflows, define trigger (e.g., push), set up Python, install dependencies, run tests.
questions.explanation: Define workflow YAML, specify trigger, set up environment, install, and test.

questions.question: What is a primary benefit of technical mentorship for the mentee?
options: B. Accelerated learning and career growth
questions.correct_answer: B. Accelerated learning and career growth
questions.explanation: Mentorship provides guidance, accelerating skill development and career progression.

questions.question: Which quality is most crucial for an effective technical mentor?
options: A. Being the most senior developer
C. Ability to write all code for the mentee
D. Strict adherence to personal coding style
questions.explanation: Effective mentors communicate clearly and understand mentee's needs.

questions.question: A good technical mentor provides constructive ___ to help mentees improve their skills.
questions.explanation: Constructive feedback is vital for mentee's growth and skill development.

questions.question: How would you approach mentoring a junior developer struggling with a complex technical problem?
questions.correct_answer: Listen, ask guiding questions, break down problem, suggest resources, encourage independent problem-solving.
questions.explanation: Guide them to solutions, don't just provide answers, fostering independence.

questions.question: Systems Thinking primarily focuses on understanding:
options: B. Interconnections and relationships within a system
C. Only the output of a system
D. The fastest way to fix a bug
questions.correct_answer: B. Interconnections and relationships within a system
questions.explanation: Systems thinking emphasizes understanding how parts interact as a whole.

questions.question: Which of these is a benefit of applying Systems Thinking in software development?
options: A. Simplifies complex problems by ignoring dependencies
B. Helps anticipate unintended consequences of changes
C. Reduces the need for documentation
questions.correct_answer: B. Helps anticipate unintended consequences of changes
questions.explanation: Systems thinking helps foresee impacts of changes across the entire system.

questions.question: When debugging a distributed system, ___ Thinking helps identify root causes beyond isolated component failures.
questions.explanation: Systems Thinking helps analyze interactions and dependencies for root causes.

questions.question: How does Systems Thinking help in designing a resilient and scalable microservices architecture?
questions.correct_answer: It helps understand service interactions, data flow, failure modes, and overall system behavior for robustness.
questions.explanation: It considers interdependencies, failure propagation, and emergent behaviors for robustness.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the OWASP Top 10?
options: A. To provide a list of secure programming languages
B. To identify the 10 most critical web application security risks
C. To offer a certification for cybersecurity professionals
D. To publish daily security news updates
questions.correct_answer: B. To identify the 10 most critical web application security risks
questions.explanation: OWASP Top 10 lists critical web application security risks for awareness.

questions.question: Which OWASP Top 10 vulnerability involves injecting malicious code into a web page viewed by other users?
questions.explanation: XSS allows attackers to inject client-side scripts into web pages.

questions.question: OWASP recommends using parameterized queries or ORMs to prevent ___ Injection vulnerabilities.
questions.explanation: Parameterized queries prevent SQL Injection by separating code from data.

questions.question: Explain the concept of 'Broken Access Control' from the OWASP Top 10 and how to mitigate it.
questions.correct_answer: Users accessing unauthorized functions/data. Mitigate with robust authorization checks, least privilege, and deny-by-default.
questions.explanation: Broken Access Control allows unauthorized access. Implement strict authorization checks.

questions.question: What does HAZOP stand for in safety engineering?
questions.explanation: Systematic hazard identification method.

questions.question: Which international standard primarily focuses on functional safety of electrical/electronic/programmable electronic safety-related systems?
questions.explanation: Key standard for functional safety.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of a Layer of Protection Analysis (LOPA)?
options: A. To identify all potential hazards
B. To quantify risk and assess protection layers
C. To develop emergency response plans
D. To train personnel on safety procedures
questions.correct_answer: B. To quantify risk and assess protection layers
questions.explanation: Evaluates independent protection layers.

questions.question: Which document typically outlines procedures for responding to accidents or emergencies?
questions.explanation: Guides actions during emergencies.

questions.question: What is a common contributing factor to industrial accidents?
questions.explanation: Often a significant factor.

questions.question: Which safety measure prevents unexpected startup of machinery during maintenance?
questions.explanation: Ensures energy isolation.

questions.question: A ___________ is a systematic process for identifying hazards and evaluating risks.
questions.explanation: Identifies and assesses risks.

questions.question: The acronym PPE stands for ___________ ___________ ___________.
questions.explanation: Protects individuals from hazards.

questions.question: Safety Data Sheets (SDS) provide crucial information on ___________ materials.
questions.explanation: Details safe handling of substances.

questions.question: Regular ___________ are crucial for maintaining safety awareness and competence.
questions.explanation: Keeps knowledge current.

questions.question: Incident investigation aims to determine the ___________ cause of an event.
questions.explanation: Finds underlying problem source.

questions.question: Explain the importance of fostering a strong safety culture within an organization.
questions.explanation: Promotes proactive hazard identification and prevention.

questions.question: Describe the typical steps involved in responding to a workplace incident.
questions.explanation: Secure scene, aid injured, investigate, report.

questions.question: How do you ensure ongoing compliance with relevant safety regulations and standards?
questions.explanation: Regular audits, training, documentation, updates.

questions.question: What is the primary role and benefit of implementing a Safety Management System (SMS)?
questions.explanation: Systematic approach to managing safety risks.

questions.question: Which diagramming tool is commonly used to visualize system components and their interactions?
questions.explanation: Standard for software system modeling.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of conducting a Root Cause Analysis (RCA)?
options: B. To identify underlying problems, not just symptoms
questions.correct_answer: B. To identify underlying problems, not just symptoms
questions.explanation: Finds fundamental reasons for issues.

questions.question: Which method helps prioritize requirements based on their importance?
questions.explanation: Categorizes requirements: Must, Should, Could, Won't.

questions.question: What does FMEA stand for in the context of technical analysis?
options: A. Functional Model and Evaluation Approach
B. Failure Mode and Effects Analysis
C. Feature Management and Engineering Assessment
D. Fault Mitigation and Error Avoidance
questions.correct_answer: B. Failure Mode and Effects Analysis
questions.explanation: Identifies potential failures and impacts.

questions.question: Which type of analysis focuses on how well a system performs under various conditions?
questions.explanation: Evaluates speed, responsiveness, stability.

questions.question: What is a key output document from the requirements gathering phase of a project?
questions.explanation: Details what the system must do.

questions.question: ___________ analysis evaluates system behavior under extreme loads or stress conditions.
questions.explanation: Tests system limits.

questions.question: A ___________ diagram visually represents the sequence of operations or decisions.
questions.explanation: Shows process flow.

questions.question: ___________ testing verifies if the system meets the end-users' needs and expectations.
questions.explanation: Confirms user satisfaction.

questions.question: Technical analysis often involves breaking down complex systems into smaller, manageable ___________.
questions.explanation: Simplifies complex systems.

questions.question: The goal of ___________ analysis is to optimize the utilization of available resources.
questions.explanation: Maximizes efficiency of assets.

questions.question: Explain the fundamental difference between functional and non-functional requirements.
questions.explanation: Functional: what system does; Non-functional: how well.

questions.question: Describe the key steps involved in conducting a comprehensive stakeholder analysis for a new project.
questions.explanation: Identify, categorize, understand influence and needs.

questions.question: What are the primary benefits of utilizing modeling tools (e.g., UML) in technical analysis?
questions.explanation: Clarity, consistency, early defect detection.

questions.question: How do you effectively validate technical specifications to ensure accuracy and completeness?
questions.explanation: Reviews, walkthroughs, prototyping, testing.

tasks.title: Safety Protocol Audit & Report
tasks.description: Conduct a mock safety audit for a hypothetical manufacturing system. Identify potential hazards, non-compliance issues, and propose corrective actions. Document findings in a formal report.
requirements: Research common industrial safety protocols (e.g., OSHA, ISO 45001).
Define a scope for your hypothetical system.
Use analytical thinking to identify risks.
tasks.expected_output: A comprehensive audit report detailing findings, risk levels, and actionable recommendations for safety improvement.

tasks.title: System Requirements Document (SRD) Development
tasks.description: Draft a System Requirements Document (SRD) for a new automated inventory management system. Include functional, non-functional, and interface requirements.
requirements: Research best practices for technical writing and SRD structure.
Apply technical analysis techniques to define system needs.
Consider potential user roles and system interactions.
tasks.expected_output: A well-structured SRD document (minimum 10 pages) outlining all necessary system requirements.

tasks.title: CAD Design for a System Component
tasks.description: Using a CAD software (e.g., FreeCAD, AutoCAD trial), design a simple mechanical component (e.g., a bracket, a housing) that would be part of a larger system. Focus on basic dimensions and tolerances.
requirements: Familiarity with basic CAD software interface and tools.
Analytical thinking to determine component fit and function.
Understanding of basic engineering drawing principles.
tasks.expected_output: A 3D CAD model file of the designed component and a 2D technical drawing with key dimensions.

tasks.title: ISO 9001 Compliance Review
tasks.description: Select a common business process (e.g., 'product development' or 'customer support') and analyze it against the core principles and requirements of ISO 9001:2015. Identify gaps and suggest improvements.
requirements: Access to ISO 9001:2015 standard documentation (or summary).
Apply analytical thinking to process evaluation.
Ability to articulate compliance gaps and solutions.
tasks.expected_output: A report detailing the process analysis, identified ISO 9001 compliance gaps, and proposed corrective actions.

tasks.title: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA)
tasks.description: Perform an FMEA on a critical function of a hypothetical system (e.g., the 'power supply' of a server rack or the 'braking system' of a vehicle). Identify potential failure modes, their causes, effects, and severity.
requirements: Analytical thinking to foresee failure scenarios.
Basic knowledge of system components and interactions.
tasks.expected_output: A completed FMEA table for the chosen system function, including recommended mitigation strategies.

roadmap.task: Introduction to industrial safety, key regulations (OSHA, IEC 61508 overview), and hazard identification methods (HAZOP).
Deep dive into risk assessment methodologies, LOPA, incident investigation, and emergency response planning.

Introduction to system modeling (UML diagrams), Root Cause Analysis (RCA), and Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA).
Develop critical thinking skills, problem-solving frameworks, data interpretation, and decision-making techniques.
Overview of ISO 9001 (Quality Management System), its principles, and application in system engineering.
Introduction to ISO 27001 (Information Security) and ISO 45001 (Occupational Health & Safety) relevant to systems.
Learn principles of clear, concise technical communication, document structuring, and audience awareness.
Practice writing technical specifications, reports, user manuals, and project documentation with clarity.
Hands-on introduction to a CAD software (e.g., FreeCAD, Fusion 360) for basic 2D sketching and 3D modeling of components.

questions.question: What does LOTO stand for in industrial safety?

questions.question: The Pareto Principle suggests that what percentage of effects come from 20% of causes?
